description_long: This street cabinet is a rectangular, metal enclosure mounted on a concrete base, commonly found in urban environments for housing electrical or communication equipment. The cabinet features a textured, matte gray finish with visible weathering and minor surface imperfections, indicating outdoor use. On one narrow side, there is a small access panel, while the opposite side has a larger door with two visible fasteners, likely for maintenance access. The top surface is flat with a single, dome-shaped protrusion, possibly a vent or sensor. The main faces are largely unadorned, with no visible signage or warning labels. The overall construction appears robust and tamper-resistant, suitable for protecting sensitive infrastructure components.
description: A robust, gray metal street cabinet on a concrete base with a flat top and a dome-shaped protrusion, featuring access doors on the narrow sides.
description_view_0: Narrow side view showing a small rectangular access panel near the top of the cabinet.
description_view_1: Wide face with a plain, textured surface and no visible doors or features except for weathering.
description_view_2: Opposite narrow side with a larger access door secured by two fasteners, mounted on the concrete base.
description_view_3: Wide face with a central seam and a single fastener, indicating a main access door.
